common.apiDataFor: API reports data for: {{periods}} — switch period above if available.
common.countsCcc: Counts consecutive sampled UTC days with negative close-to-close change.
common.largestDrop: Largest point-to-point drop on the selected curve window.
common.externalOrCurve: External total return or curve ratio
common.pnlFromStart: Profit / loss from the selected window start
common.worstRunningDrawdown: Worst running-peak drawdown in this window (chart shows full path)
common.benchmarkPnl: Benchmark: 0 profit / loss at window start
common.recoveryHint: Drawdown at now minus worst in window (percentage points).
common.noCurveBody: This wallet does not have enough curve points for the selected period yet.

footer.tagline: Polymarket smart money copy trading — discover, backtest, and copy with risk controls.

support.title: Support
support.panelHint: We usually reply during business hours.

hero.title: Copy the Smartest Traders on Polymarket
hero.subtitle: Discover profitable Polymarket wallets, backtest their performance, and copy trades with risk controls.

heroMock.aria: Dashboard preview with top trader, backtest, and copy settings

leaderboard.title: Rank wallets by signal quality, not hype.
leaderboard.body: CopyOdds ranks Polymarket traders using win rate, PnL, activity, holdings, and risk flags — so you can shortlist wallets worth a deeper look before copying.

backtest.title: See the full performance picture.
backtest.body: Review historical return, drawdown, trade frequency, and consistency before you mirror a wallet. Backtests help separate durable edge from short-term luck.
backtest.insight: A smooth equity curve with contained drawdowns usually signals copy-worthy discipline — not just a lucky streak.

copy.title: Copy trades with guardrails built in.
copy.body: Set max position size, per-market caps, copy limits, and wallet protection before any trade is mirrored. You choose the rules — CopyOdds enforces them on every copy action.

features.title: Max position size
features.body: Cap how much of your balance can be allocated to any single mirrored trade.

features.title: Per-market cap
features.body: Limit total mirrored notional per prediction market within a day so one theme cannot dominate your exposure.

features.title: Copy limits
features.body: Set total copy budget and per-wallet limits so exposure stays within your plan.

features.title: Wallet protection
features.body: Non-custodial architecture with passkey and session controls to protect account access.

howItWorks.title: From discovery to automated copy in four steps

steps.title: Discover traders
steps.body: Browse the smart money leaderboard and filter wallets by performance, activity, and risk.

steps.title: Backtest performance
steps.body: Inspect return curves, drawdowns, and trade history before committing capital.

steps.title: Set risk controls
steps.body: Configure max position, per-market caps, copy limits, and wallet protection for each subscription.

steps.title: Copy automatically
steps.body: When a followed wallet trades, CopyOdds mirrors the action within your risk rules.

why.title: Not every profitable wallet is worth copying.
why.body: CopyOdds helps users evaluate consistency, risk, activity, and copy suitability — not just headline PnL — so copy decisions are informed, not impulsive.

points.title: Consistency over spikes
points.body: Surface wallets with repeatable performance instead of one-off winning streaks.

points.title: Risk-aware ranking
points.body: Risk flags and drawdown context sit beside returns so you see the full profile.

points.title: Activity that matters
points.body: Distinguish active traders from stale wallets that no longer reflect live strategy.

points.title: Copy suitability
points.body: Backtests and rule presets help you decide whether a wallet fits your risk budget.

cta.title: Start with the Smart Money Leaderboard

hero.body: CopyOdds turns a noisy wallet leaderboard into a tighter research view. Scan who the trader is, why the profile stands out, and how strong the current signal looks before you click through.
hero.cleanerBody: Less table noise and fewer raw fields fighting for attention.
hero.sharperBody: Better hierarchy for score, conviction, consistency, and risk.
hero.fasterBody: Click any trader name to jump straight to the official Polymarket profile.
hero.filterBody: The board starts by favoring cleaner, more display-worthy profiles instead of dumping every scored wallet at once.
hero.whyFollowBody: Stronger summaries highlight why a trader is interesting before you decode the raw score stack.
hero.officialBody: Trader names link directly to Polymarket so you can verify the profile in the native source.
hero.researchBody: This page should help you shortlist faster, not replace deeper diligence.

home.metricTopScoreWaiting: Waiting for the first ranked trader
home.metricEligibleHint: Across the rows loaded so far
home.methodWhyTitle: Not just profit. Quality and repeatability.
home.methodWhyBody: This board blends signal quality, activity, consistency, and risk penalties so the list feels like a research surface instead of a raw admin dump.
home.methodReadBody: Each row focuses on the trader name, the strongest reasons to follow, and a compact metric stack, without exposing raw wallet strings in the UI.
home.methodRiskTitle: Useful for research, not blind copying.
home.methodRiskBody: Even strong wallets rotate strategy, underperform, or carry stale data. Use the board to narrow the field, then verify on the official profile page.

leaderboard.title: Smart money profiles worth a closer look.
leaderboard.subtitle: Identity first, reasons second, metrics third — scroll the list or load more rows when you need a wider slice of the board.
leaderboard.filterGroupAria: Month and week filter the candidate pool; All time uses system ranking
leaderboard.titleAll: All time uses system ranking; no month/week candidate filter
leaderboard.titlePeriod: Only wallets that entered the “{{label}}” candidate pool, sorted by rank within that window
leaderboard.loadingBody: Pulling the latest cached smart money rows from the backend.
leaderboard.emptyBody: The cached leaderboard is empty — check back after the next sync.

leaderboardTable.rankTitle: List position in the current sort (starts at 1)
leaderboardTable.openXTitle: Open X profile on x.com (new tab)

coverage.noTsPoints: No time-series points for this window

backtest.polygonscanAria: View this address on Polygonscan — Polymarket activity is mostly on Polygon (opens in new tab)
backtest.performanceBody: Interactive profit/loss curve aligned with Polymarket's chart presentation. The dashed line marks zero profit/loss at the selected window start.
backtest.windowOverviewBody: The page now uses the upgraded risk profile response directly: curve coverage, backtest rollups, and freshness metadata all come from the same API payload.
backtest.curveTypeHint: Profit / loss relative to the selected window start
backtest.largestGainHint: Largest point-to-point gain on the displayed curve
backtest.largestDropHint: Largest point-to-point drop on the displayed curve
backtest.analystEmpty: Once curve points are available, this block summarizes stability, downside, and sample quality for the selected window.
backtest.statsBody: Derived from the selected curve only. Daily buckets use the first and last observed point within each UTC day, plus intraday peak-to-trough scan.
backtest.statsEmptyCurve: This response has an empty curve.points array, so nothing here can be computed from a time series. Use external / snapshot blocks above until the backend merges usable curve samples.
backtest.worstDayNeed: Need at least one sampled day with multiple curve points.
backtest.bestDayNeed: Need at least one sampled day with multiple curve points.
backtest.intradayNeed: Requires intraday curve points within a sampled UTC day.
backtest.rollingNeed: Need sampled daily buckets to evaluate rolling windows.
backtest.positiveDaysNeed: Daily ratio appears once enough curve points are available.
backtest.followBody: Illustrative outcomes from the same curve window — not live copy-trading execution.
backtest.riskBody: Local curve risk metrics are primary. When history is thin, external metrics stay visible as a secondary reference instead of replacing the main read.
backtest.frequencyFootCurve: Curve span ≈ {{days}} days · counts are snapshot-level, not intraday fills.
backtest.frequencyFootSnapshot: Snapshot-level activity; intraday fills are not modeled here.
backtest.candidateBody: Signals whether this wallet showed up in weekly, monthly, or all-time candidate sources.
backtest.metricsNoTopic: Topic-level market mix is not available from this endpoint.
backtest.loadingBody: Fetching the cached smart money curve, summary metrics, and risk breakdown for this wallet.
backtest.errorPageTitle: Unable to load this backtest page.
backtest.copyResultCurve: Over {{period}}, mirrored portfolio PnL {{value}} on this curve window (not execution-level copy).
backtest.copyResultSnapshot: No curve in this response; Polymarket snapshot total PnL is {{value}} (board / external metrics below are independent of the empty curve array).
backtest.copyResultNone: Not enough curve points to summarize a copy outcome for this period.
backtest.hintWindowPnl: Window PnL {{value}} from the selected start point
backtest.hintLargeExternal: Large values are external-style return scores, not a PnL % from this curve.
backtest.hintCurveProxy: Curve-based return proxy for this window
backtest.noteNoCurve: No portfolio curve for {{period}}; chart and step backtest need points. External metrics ({{source}}) can still populate.
backtest.noteFallback: Requested {{requested}} had no curve points; the chart uses {{active}} instead (server fallback). External metrics still follow {{requested}}.
backtest.noteReturn: {{period}} window return proxy {{ret}} with smart score {{score}}.
backtest.noteStable: The curve looks relatively stable: more green days than red and drawdown stayed contained.
backtest.noteRough: Expect a rougher ride here: downside excursions are material and risk tolerance matters.
backtest.notePartial: Requested period is only partially covered, so this view is directionally useful but not complete.
backtest.noteThinSample: Daily backtest sample is still thin; treat streaks and day ratios as early signals, not hard conclusions.
backtest.noteSampled: Backtest summary is based on {{count}} sampled UTC days from the same displayed curve.
backtest.curveEmptyFlag: Curve points array empty in API
